download_details.title: Readiness to regulate small modular reactors
The Federal Authority to License Small Modular Reactors – Institute for Policy Integrity

findings: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has legal authority under the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to license SMRs as 'utilization facilities,' and the 2024 ADVANCE Act confirms Congress understood the NRC to have this licensing authority.

The question of whether SMRs qualify as 'utilization facilities' under the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 is at the heart of recent litigation and determines whether the NRC has authority to license them at all.

High capital costs and lengthy licensing and approval processes have limited the expansion of nuclear power in the United States, where electric utilities operate about 98 GW of nuclear generating capacity.

Several SMR vendors with designs not readily classified in standard reactor categories are engaged in pre-application activities with the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), indicating NRC pre-application engagement is part of the path to approval.

The DOE's Energy Reactor Pilot Program, announced in June 2025, aims to expedite testing of advanced reactor designs and provide a fast-track approach to licensing, with applicants responsible for funding their individual pilot reactor designs.
